name: Hauts-de-France (France)

colors.symbolism: the silver (argent) field of the historic arms of Flanders
the gold (or) field of the historic arms of Picardy
the lion of Flanders (sable lion rampant)
the tongue and claws of the lion, and the gules elements of Picardy

description: A field divided per bend sinister: the upper-hoist white half charged with the black lion of Flanders, and the lower-fly gold half charged with red Picardy heraldry.
history: Hauts-de-France is a French region created by the territorial reform of 2016, merging Nord-Pas-de-Calais and Picardy. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and this composite armorial banner is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted regional flag.

features.symbolism: A field divided per bend sinister: the upper-hoist triangle is white (or argent) and bears the black lion of Flanders / Nord-Pas-de-Calais; the lower-fly triangle is gold (or) and bears the red attributes of Picardy.

features.role: lion of Flanders (sable lion rampant, armed and langued gules)
features.symbolism: The black lion of Flanders is the historic emblem of the County of Flanders and of the modern departement of Nord-Pas-de-Calais.

features.role: the historic arms of Picardy on the gold half
features.symbolism: Charges from the historic arms of Picardy, the southern half of the modern Hauts-de-France region.
